stories.headline: Joe Rogan reacts to Talmud quote on self-pleasure with Tom Segura
stories.body:
Joe Rogan left in shock with Tom Segura after reading what the Jewish Bible says about self-pleasure:

ROGAN: “The Talmud says crazy sh*t about how you can impregnate a demon from a different dimension.”

SEGURA: “That’s dark. They only said that to get people to stop touching themselves.”

ROGAN: “People are guilty of doing that today more than ever before because we have phones.”

SEGURA: “There are psychos who will spend hours stopping before they finish so they can keep going.”

ROGAN: “They’re waiting for the right scene to end their session on.”

SEGURA: “Have you ever done that?”

ROGAN: “I’ve done it and then hated myself for hours afterwards.”

stories.headline: Sunday meal prep recipes for 1000g protein including chili and chicken soup
stories.body:
It’s Sunday, which means it’s time to set yourself up for a week of healthy eating

Here's how you can prep 1,000g of protein in less than 30 minutes of prep time

What you’ll need:

Chuck chili:
-5 lb chuck (cubed) or stew meat (I prefer this because it's already cubed; one less step)
-2 cups beef bone broth
-2 14oz cans tomato sauce
-2 packets chili seasoning

Chicken soup:
-5 lb chicken breasts or thighs
-10 carrots
-1 yellow onion
-5 celery sticks
-4 cloves garlic
-1 bunch fresh dill
-2 bay leaves
-8-10 cups chicken bone broth
-Olive or avocado oil
-Optional: spinach or bok
-Optional: rice noodles
-Salt and pepper

Start by pre heating your oven to 325. Since both meals will be cooking at once, we are going to do the chicken soup on the stove top and the chuck in the oven.

Start by prepping all of your ingredients. To make the cooking process more smooth, I always recommend doing ALL of the prep and organize before you turn the heat on.

So start by chopping your carrots, celery, onions, garlic, and dill (if you are using chuck instead of stew meat you can go ahead and dice that too) into bite sized pieces. This is the only prep you’ll need to do!

Since the chuck takes longer, we’ll start with that.

Add the bone broth, tomato sauce, chili seasoning over medium high heat and bring to a simmer, mixing well.

Once it’s starting to bubble add your chuck. Place in the oven and cook for around 3 hours (or until the meat is fork tender).

Yes, you can elevate this dish a little bit (I was just going for speed)

Optional step: sear your meat first, then add chopped onions and garlic, THEN add the broth/tomato sauce/chili seasoning/chuck.

I like to serve this in a bowl with rice, white onion and cilantro, but you can also do a taco!

Next is the chicken soup:

In a large pot over medium heat, add a dash of oil and your carrots, onions, celery and a pinch of salt. Saute for 5 minutes or so.

Add the garlic and bay leaves, and toast for 1 minute.

Add the chicken bone broth, mix well and bring to a simmer.

Once it’s simmering add your chicken, and simmer away until your chicken easily shreds (around 30-40 minutes).

Remove all of the chicken, shred it up, and add back to the soup. Add your fresh dill, taste for salt and you are done!

If you are adding spinach or bok choy you can add this in the last couple minutes.

I like to serve this with Jovial brown rice pasta.

Some notes:
-You can totally customize these recipes however you want!
-Yes, the recipes take some time to cook, but very little active time. Most of it is just letting it simmer away!
-I like the balance of lean (chicken breast) and fattier (chuck) for a day of eating. This is a great way to get enough fat but not overdo it (especially if you are trying to lose weight)
-Both of these recipes also have a great amino acid balance with the muscle meat and bone broth
-These freeze very well! You could make both of these and freeze half, then have a weeks worth of meals for the future
-These are both one pot meals so cleanup is minimal
-If you don’t want to make this much, you can just cut both recipes in half!

Let me know if you have any questions!

stories.headline: MiHoYo AI agents experiment burns $300K in tokens
stories.body:
MiHoYo burned through nearly $300,000 worth of AI tokens in just 13 hours during an internal AI experiment.

The story came from Zheng Yinhe, head of the AI NPC speaking at the 2026 Alibaba Cloud Summit. 

According to him, an employee set up dozens of AI agents to work together on a project. The agents kept talking to each other, generating outputs, and reviewing each other’s work, causing token usage to skyrocket overnight.

The expensive experiment didn’t pay off. Despite the massive cost, the final results were reportedly full of hallucinations and unusable.
